mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over recent months, the discount rate on future earnings declines and equity valuations re-rate upward. This predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, with the signal strongest when inflation is not accelerating at the same time.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly declines in FEDFUNDS do not precede positive 20-day SPX returns more often than 50% of cases, or if the sign of the forward-return effect is negative in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A labor-market deterioration signal emerges when UNRATE rises sharply above its recent trend. That threshold marks rising recession risk and weaker earnings expectations, which should produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered events are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average 5-day return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both accelerate together, it implies broad demand growth with improving payroll income and sales momentum. That joint expansion should lift near-term equity returns over the next 20 trading days through higher revenue expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform single-series effects, or if periods with simultaneous RSAFS and PAYEMS acceleration have negative average 20-day SPX returns across any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A falling INDPRO reading signals weakening industrial activity and softer cyclical demand. When that decline is present, investors should mark down earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative monthly changes in INDPRO are not associated with below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the effect flips positive in a sustained 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts collapse below trend signals tightening financial conditions and weakening household demand. Once HOUST is materially below its recent average, equity returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days as recession odds rise.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10% below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if threshold breaches are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the average post-trigger 60-day return is not statistically below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of elevated CPIAUCSL and restrictive FEDFUNDS identifies real-rate pressure and a policy stance that compresses risk appetite. If CPI is rising while policy remains high, S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-policy condition fails to reduce 20-day SPX returns relative to either series alone, or if triggered periods have nonnegative average returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, falling PAYEMS after prior gains signals labor-market fatigue before demand softening appears in other series. That deterioration should lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the trigger has no edge versus unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth and retail sales both strengthen, final demand is broadening and corporate revenue expectations improve. That combination should support positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, especially after revisions confirm the acceleration.
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent GDPC1 and RSAFS strength does not outperform either series alone, or if the joint signal is associated with negative 60-day SPX returns in any two consecutive quarters.
